description:
## Background
A hard no-useEffect lint rule cannot land while the six existing direct calls remain: the rule setup commit itself would fail the repository checks. We need the policy in place first without making the current tree unbuildable or unlintable.

## What to build
Configure Oxlint `no-restricted-syntax` through the ESLint JS plugin to report direct React `useEffect` calls as a non-blocking warning. The diagnostic should explain the approved replacement patterns and identify the intentional `useMountEffect` escape hatch, if one is introduced.

## Technical Approach
Add the rule at warning severity while the existing call sites are being migrated. Keep any lint suppression narrowly scoped to the `useMountEffect` implementation itself. Do not add per-call-site suppressions or allow the current effects to become permanent exceptions. Verify that the TypeScript/Vite build remains clean and that the staged lint configuration can be committed before migrations begin.

## Considerations
The project currently has six direct calls across App, Mermaid, Issue Explorer, and app-settings. The warning is a temporary migration guard, not the final policy. The later enforcement task must promote the rule to an error only after every migration child is complete.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Oxlint recognizes direct `useEffect` calls through the configured JS plugin rule.
- [ ] Existing direct calls are reported as warnings rather than blocking the setup change.
- [ ] The diagnostic points agents toward derived state, event handlers, data fetching, remount boundaries, or `useMountEffect` as appropriate.
- [ ] Any escape-hatch suppression is limited to the wrapper implementation.
- [ ] `pnpm build` remains green and the staged lint check does not fail solely because of the known migration warnings.

## Test Plan
- Run the relevant Oxlint/Ultracite check and inspect that all six known call sites are reported.
- Run `pnpm build`.
- Confirm no unrelated files receive lint suppressions.

Triage: ready-for-agent.
title: Introduce staged no-useEffect Oxlint rule
